skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Helps review folders, find duplicate files, propose better structures, and organize files after user approval.
skill.summary: Organize files and folders with analysis, duplicate review, and guided cleanup plans.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution, but no Ruby execution was found in SKILL.md. The skill does instruct agents to run shell commands and move files, so it is a legitimate productivity skill with medium operational risk. Weak crypto and reconnaissance alerts at the reported lines are false positives; the only MD5 usage is for duplicate detection, not security.

medium_findings.title: Shell Commands Operate on User-Selected Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell command examples using placeholders such as target directories. This is legitimate for file organization, but direct substitution of unsafe paths could cause command errors or shell injection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documented examples, not hidden executable code. Risk remains because they are intended to be adapted to user-provided filesystem paths.

medium_findings.title: File Moves and Deletion Workflow Can Be Destructive
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to move, rename, archive, and potentially delete files after approval. This can affect user data if the plan is wrong or confirmation is misunderstood.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file modification workflow is explicit and central to the skill. The skill also includes approval safeguards, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: MD5 Used Only for Duplicate Detection
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts are false positives for the reported lines. The only clear MD5 command is used to group likely duplicate files, not to protect secrets or verify trust.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding instructions describe exact duplicate detection. No evidence found that MD5 is used for authentication, signing, encryption, or security decisions.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines contain examples about Downloads, naming, and Desktop cleanup. No evidence found of network scanning, host discovery, credential collection, or system profiling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain file-organization guidance. They do not contain network commands, host enumeration commands, or credential handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples for cd, ls, find, du, md5, mkdir, and mv. These commands are expected for this skill but require path quoting and user review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown examples and match the skill purpose. They are not covert execution, but they can change local files.

content.value_statement: Messy folders make files hard to find and waste storage. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code analyze folders, find duplicates, and propose safer cleanup plans.
actual_capabilities: Reviews folder contents and summarizes file types, sizes, and structure.
Finds likely duplicate files by hash, name, and file size.
Suggests folder structures based on file type, purpose, and date.
Creates cleanup plans before moving or renaming files.
Provides maintenance tips after an organization task.
limitations: Requires filesystem access to inspect or modify local folders.
Can move or rename files only after clear user approval.
Duplicate detection examples use MD5 for convenience, not security verification.
Cannot infer sensitive context from file names with perfect accuracy.

use_cases.title: Clean a Downloads Folder
use_cases.description: Sort mixed downloads into documents, images, installers, archives, and items needing review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Projects for Archiving
use_cases.description: Separate active projects from older work and propose an archive structure.

use_cases.title: Review Shared Team Folders
use_cases.description: Identify duplicate files, inconsistent names, and folder structure problems before cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Folder Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Downloads folder and summarize what types of files are there. Do not move or delete anything yet.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a safe first look at a messy folder.

prompt_templates.title: Duplicate File Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Find likely duplicate files in this folder and explain which copy I should keep. Ask before deleting anything.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to reduce storage without losing files.

prompt_templates.title: Project Folder Redesign
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Projects folder and propose a cleaner structure for active work, archives, templates, and learning projects.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer wants a better project directory layout.

prompt_templates.title: Approved Cleanup Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the approved plan to create folders and move files. Log each change and stop if any filename conflict appears.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user has reviewed the plan and wants execution.

output_examples.output: A folder analysis with file counts, suggested categories, uncertain files, and a proposed structure for user approval.
A duplicate review listing each duplicate group, recommended copy to keep, and deletion questions for each group.
A project structure report that separates active projects, archived work, templates, and folders needing manual decisions.

best_practices: Start with one folder and review the analysis before making changes.
Require an explicit plan before moving, renaming, archiving, or deleting files.
Keep a move log so changes can be reviewed or reversed.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad cleanup across the whole home directory without narrowing scope.
Do not delete duplicates until the user confirms each deletion group.
Do not substitute untrusted paths into shell commands without quoting and review.

faq.question: Does this skill delete files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The instructions require confirmation before deletion and recommend review before changes.

faq.question: Can it organize photos by date?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose year and month folders using EXIF data or modification dates.

faq.question: Can it find duplicate files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare hashes, names, and sizes to find likely duplicates.

faq.question: Is MD5 used for security?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses MD5 only as a duplicate detection example.

faq.question: Can it organize project directories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest active, archive, template, and learning folders.

faq.question: What should users review before approving changes?
faq.answer: Users should review target folders, proposed moves, rename rules, deletion candidates, and conflict handling.
